decision.resolution: R/ggplot2 is PRIMARY for publication figures. matplotlib kept for quick diagnostics only (not manuscript). Rationale: user explicitly requested ggplot2 matching foundation-PLR, and biomedical audience expects R-style figures. Existing biostatistics_figures.py becomes a fallback/diagnostic tool.
DELETED. DuckDB is the ONLY input contract. Analysis flow materializes DuckDB; biostatistics flow reads DuckDB. No mlruns scanning, no Parquet bypass.

Stratified within-fold permutation test (volume labels shuffled within each fold stratum). This is the primary analysis. Fold-level aggregation is a sensitivity check. Per DiCiccio & Efron (1996): percentile bootstrap for N < 20, BCa for N >= 20.

Random intercept only: metric ~ C(model) * C(loss) * C(calib) + (1|fold). statsmodels.mixedlm for Python pathway; lmerTest::anova with Satterthwaite for R pathway (primary for manuscript tables). Partial eta-squared approximated via Nakagawa & Schielzeth (2013) marginal/conditional R-squared.

Git tag `pre-registration/mini-experiment-v1` created after Phase 0, before Phase 3. Freezes: factorial YAML, split config, biostatistics config, analysis plan. Timestamped commit is the pre-registration artifact.

phase.name: Mini-Experiment Design
phase.description: Design minimal factorial subset exercising the full Layer A-D pipeline locally. Clean slate — no legacy data, no metric migration. Per-volume metrics from day one.

task.name: Define factorial cells
task.spec:
Layer A (Training, GPU): model_family: [dynunet] (1 model — sufficient for pipeline testing) loss_name: [dice_ce, cbdice_cldice] (2 losses — tests the comparison machinery) aux_calibration: [false] (1 level — calibration deferred to full factorial) → 2 Layer A cells
Layer B (Post-Training, CPU): method: [none, checkpoint_averaging] (2 methods) recalibration: [none] (1 level) → 2 Layer B cells
Layer C (Analysis, CPU): ensemble_strategy: [per_loss_single_best] (1 strategy) → 1 Layer C cell
Total conditions: 2 × 2 × 1 = 4 conditions Folds: 3 (full CV, seed=42, configs/splits/3fold_seed42.json) Epochs: 20 (mini — enough for meaningful metrics, fast enough for local) Validation volumes: 23 per fold, 70 total across folds External test: DeepVess 7 volumes
Per-volume metric logging: eval/{fold}/vol/{vol_id}/dsc eval/{fold}/vol/{vol_id}/cldice eval/{fold}/vol/{vol_id}/masd test/deepvess/vol/{vol_id}/dsc test/deepvess/vol/{vol_id}/cldice test/deepvess/vol/{vol_id}/masd

task.name: Create smoke_mini factorial YAML
task.spec: configs/factorial/smoke_mini.yaml — derived from pre-gcp-master-plan.xml configs/biostatistics/smoke_mini.yaml — reads smoke_mini_evaluation experiment Reduced n_bootstrap: 500 (speed), full metrics list

task.name: Create pre-registration git tag
task.spec: After factorial YAML + biostatistics config + split config committed: git tag -a pre-registration/mini-experiment-v1 -m "Pre-registration: factorial design frozen" This tag is the pre-registration artifact for Nature Protocols TRIPOD+AI Item 3.

phase.name: Statistical Audit and Fix
phase.description: Fix all statistical correctness issues identified by council biostatistician. Every fix has a deterministic test with known statistical outcomes. TDD: RED (failing test) → GREEN (implement fix) → VERIFY.

task.name: Fix _pool_scores() — stratified permutation
task.spec:
PROBLEM: Current code (biostatistics_statistics.py lines 294-297) does np.concatenate across folds, treating clustered observations as i.i.d. This violates exchangeability and inflates effective sample size.
FIX: Primary analysis = stratified within-fold permutation test. - Join on (fold_id, volume_id) to guarantee pairing across conditions - Permute condition labels WITHIN each fold stratum - Combine per-stratum test statistics via Fisher's method or Stouffer's Z - Sensitivity check: fold-level aggregation (mean per fold, then test on K=3)
SPECIFICATION: "Primary analysis: stratified within-fold permutation (volume labels shuffled within each fold stratum). Sensitivity: fold-level aggregation. The stratified test is preferred because K=3 folds provide insufficient combinatorial resolution for the fold-level test."
TEST: Synthetic data with known effect (condition A = condition B + 0.05). Assert stratified test detects it at alpha=0.05 with > 80% power over 1000 seeds.

task.name: Fix BCa threshold
task.spec:
PROBLEM: BCa bootstrap requires N >= 20 for reliable jackknife acceleration. Per DiCiccio & Efron (1996, Statistical Science).
FIX: If N < 20, use percentile bootstrap. If N >= 20, use BCa. Document threshold in BiostatisticsConfig with field: bca_min_n = 20. Per-volume analysis (N=23 within fold) → BCa OK. Fold-level aggregation (N=3) → percentile only.
TEST: Assert percentile CI for N=3 is wider than BCa CI for N=50.

task.name: Fix mixed-effects ANOVA
task.spec:
PROBLEM: Current code uses pg.anova with fold as fixed between-subjects factor. Fold is a nuisance grouping variable, not a treatment → must be random effect.
FIX: Python pathway: statsmodels.mixedlm Formula: metric_value ~ C(model_family) * C(loss_name) * C(with_aux_calib) Random: ~1 | fold_id Method: REML Note: random intercept only (K=3 insufficient for random slopes)
R pathway (primary for manuscript tables): lmerTest::anova Formula: metric_value ~ model_family * loss_name * aux_calib + (1|fold_id) Satterthwaite degrees of freedom → proper F-tests Partial eta-squared via effectsize::eta_squared()
Effect size reporting: Nakagawa & Schielzeth (2013) marginal R² and conditional R² replace partial eta-squared from fixed-effects ANOVA.
TEST: Synthetic balanced data with known factor effects. Assert F-test p-values match within 0.01 between Python and R pathways.

task.name: Implement hierarchical gatekeeping
task.spec:
Co-primaries (clDice, MASD): alpha/2 = 0.025 each (Bonferroni). Conditional on at least one co-primary rejection: Secondary metrics (DSC, HD95, etc.): BH-FDR at alpha=0.05. Per Dmitrienko et al. (2010, Statistics in Medicine). Config fields already exist: co_primary_metrics, foil_metrics.
TEST: Synthetic data where clDice is significant but MASD is not. Assert secondary tests proceed (gatekeeping passes on partial rejection).

task.name: Fix condition key parser — generic N-factor
task.spec:
PROBLEM: biostatistics_figures.py line 398-402 parses only 2 factors. 6-factor keys exist: model__loss__calib__method__recalib__ensemble.
FIX: Generic parser: split on "__", return dict of factor_name→value. Encoder: join factor values with "__". Factor values MUST NOT contain "__" — enforce with ValueError in encoder.
TEST: Hypothesis property test — round-trip encode/decode for random factor combinations. @given(st.lists(st.text(min_size=1, alphabet=SAFE_CHARS)))

task.name: Seed all random operations
task.spec:
All operations use numpy.random.Generator(PCG64(config.seed)). baycomp: check if it accepts seed param. If not, use np.random.seed() before call and document global state pollution risk in docstring. Deterministic ordering: sorted() on all dict key iterations.
TEST: Run full pipeline twice with same seed, assert bit-identical JSON sidecars.

task.name: Sensitivity concordance analysis
task.spec:
For every pairwise comparison, run BOTH parametric (paired t-test) and nonparametric (Wilcoxon signed-rank). Report concordance rate: "Two tests are concordant if they agree on significance at alpha." Flag any discordant pairs for investigation in results.
TEST: Synthetic data with known borderline effect. Verify concordance logic correctly identifies discordance when it exists.

phase.name: DuckDB Contract and Integration Tests
phase.description: Establish DuckDB as the single input contract. Create fixture DuckDB, schema contract tests, JSON sidecar schema, and Docker smoke test.

task.name: DuckDB-only refactor
task.spec: Remove mlruns re-scanning from biostatistics_flow.py _build_per_volume_data(). Read per_volume_metrics table from DuckDB exclusively. Analysis flow is responsible for materializing DuckDB from mlruns. Delete any data_source config option if it exists.

task.name: Create fixture DuckDB
task.spec:
Synthetic data: 3 folds, 4 conditions, 23 volumes/fold, 3 metrics. Known statistical outcomes: condition A is 0.03 DSC better than B (ground truth). ICC = 0.15 (moderate fold effect). Per-volume variance = 0.04. Use for: Phase 1 test validation, Phase 4 smoke test, Phase 5 R development.
SIMULATION VALIDATION: Verify pipeline recovers known effects: - Stratified Wilcoxon detects A > B at alpha=0.05 - Mixed-effects ANOVA significant main effect for loss - Power analysis returns detectable d consistent with synthetic truth This is the "positive control" — validates statistical calibration.

task.name: Schema contract tests
task.spec:
DuckDB schema validation: columns, types, non-null constraints for all 8 tables. JSON sidecar schema: Pydantic model (FigureSidecar, TableSidecar) with: - figure_id, generated_at, duckdb_sha256, git_sha, config_hash - data: Dict[str, Any] (figure-specific payload) - output_files: List[str] (referenced PDF/PNG/TEX paths) Validated in both Python (Pydantic) and R (jsonvalidate).
Tests: - test_duckdb_schema_contract.py: assert all columns, types, constraints - test_json_sidecar_schema.py: validate Pydantic model round-trip

task.name: Docker smoke test
task.spec:
Build biostatistics Docker image (multi-stage with R). Run on fixture DuckDB: docker run biostatistics /data/fixture.duckdb Assert: exit code 0, all expected output files exist, JSON sidecars valid.
Makefile target: make test-biostatistics-docker

phase.name: Run Mini-Experiment
phase.description: Train DynUNet variants locally, run post-training + analysis + DuckDB materialization. Generate real per-volume metrics for all validation and test volumes. This is the first time the full Layer A-D chain runs end-to-end.

task.name: Train Layer A (2 DynUNet × 2 losses × 3 folds)
task.spec:
Run via Prefect train flow in Docker. 6 training runs (2 losses × 3 folds), 20 epochs each. RTX 2070 Super (8 GB VRAM) — DynUNet fits comfortably. Per-volume metrics: log eval/{fold}/vol/{vol_id}/{metric} for ALL 23 val volumes. Training metrics: train/loss, val/dice, val/cldice per epoch. All factorial tags: model_family, loss_name, with_aux_calib=false, fold_id, seed. Wall-clock timing: log perf/training_seconds per run.
Estimated: ~2-4 hours for 6 runs × 20 epochs.

task.name: Run DeepVess inference
task.spec: Run inference on 7 DeepVess volumes using all 6 trained models. Log: test/deepvess/vol/{vol_id}/{metric} for each model. No training — inference only (CPU sufficient, ~5 min per model). Requires: EXTERNAL_DATA_DIR set, DeepVess data downloaded and DVC-versioned.

task.name: Run Post-Training Flow (Layer B)
task.spec: For each training run: method=none (pass-through) → tag: post_training_method=none method=checkpoint_averaging → tag: post_training_method=checkpoint_averaging Tags written: post_training_method, recalibration=none Creates: 12 post-training MLflow runs (6 training × 2 methods).

task.name: Run Analysis Flow (Layer C)
task.spec: ensemble_strategy: per_loss_single_best Evaluates all post-training models on MiniVess val + DeepVess test. Creates DuckDB: outputs/biostatistics/biostatistics.duckdb Tags: ensemble_strategy, all upstream tags inherited. Exports Parquet alongside DuckDB.

task.name: Phase 3→4 validation gate
task.spec: Run Phase 2 contract tests against the REAL DuckDB from Phase 3.4. Same test code, different fixture: pytest tests/v2/unit/biostatistics/test_duckdb_schema_contract.py \ --db-path outputs/biostatistics/biostatistics.duckdb Must pass before Phase 4 starts. If it fails, fix the upstream flow.

deliverables.artifact: mlruns/ with 6 training + 12 post-training + N analysis runs

phase.name: Biostatistics Flow End-to-End
phase.description: Run the full biostatistics flow on real data from Phase 3. DuckDB-only input. All statistical analyses, power analysis, sensitivity checks.

task.name: Run biostatistics flow
task.spec:
Input: outputs/biostatistics/biostatistics.duckdb (from Phase 3) Config: configs/biostatistics/smoke_mini.yaml Analyses: - Stratified within-fold permutation (primary pairwise) - Mixed-effects ANOVA (statsmodels + R lmerTest) - Bayesian signed-rank with ROPE (baycomp) - Variance decomposition (Friedman + ICC) - Rank concordance (Kendall's tau — DSC vs clDice inversion) - Specification curve (Simonsohn 2020) - Multi-metric rankings - Sensitivity: parametric vs nonparametric concordance
All results JSON-serializable, logged to MLflow experiment "minivess_biostatistics".

task.name: Power analysis
task.spec: Using pilot variance from mini-experiment: - Target test: stratified Wilcoxon - Effective N: N_eff = N / (1 + (n_per_fold - 1) * ICC_pilot) - Design effect correction per Killip et al. (2004) - Compute detectable Cohen's d at 80% power for each metric - Report for both per-fold (N=23) and pooled (N_eff) scenarios - Output: power_curve.pdf + power_table.tex + power_analysis.json sidecar

task.name: Multiple comparisons disclosure
task.spec: Count total tests: K_pairwise = C(4,2) × N_metrics = 6 × 14 = 84 pairwise Plus: 14 ANOVA F-tests, 14 Friedman tests, 14 spec curve permutation tests. Total: ~126 tests. Report: effective alpha after hierarchical gatekeeping + BH-FDR. Output: multiple_comparisons_summary.json

phase.name: R/ggplot2 Figure System
phase.description: Publication-quality figures via R/ggplot2 following foundation-PLR pattern. Python exports JSON/CSV → R scripts read → ggplot2 renders → PDF/PNG + JSON sidecar. Docker multi-stage build for R integration.

task.name: Docker multi-stage build with R
task.spec:
Dockerfile.biostatistics update: Stage 1 (builder): FROM rocker/r-ver:4.4.0 - renv::restore() from committed src/r/renv.lock - Packages: ggplot2, patchwork, jsonlite, ggdist, gghalves, ggrepel, scales, viridis, effectsize, lmerTest Stage 2 (runner): FROM minivess-base-cpu:latest - COPY --from=builder /usr/local/lib/R + /usr/local/bin/R* - COPY src/r/ /app/src/r/
R package version pinning via renv.lock (committed to git). docker-compose.flows.yml update: biostatistics service uses new image.

task.name: Create R figure infrastructure
task.spec:
src/r/ ├── renv.lock # Package version lock ├── figure_system/ │ ├── save_figure.R # Publication-ready save (PDF+PNG+JSON) │ ├── figure_factory.R # Core plot creation functions │ ├── config_loader.R # Load YAML configs │ ├── common.R # Shared utilities │ └── theme_vascadia.R # Custom ggplot2 theme ├── figures/ │ ├── generate_all_biostat_figures.R # Master entry point │ ├── fig_forest_plot.R │ ├── fig_effect_size_heatmap.R │ ├── fig_specification_curve.R │ ├── fig_interaction_plot.R │ ├── fig_cd_diagram.R │ ├── fig_raincloud.R │ ├── fig_calibration_reliability.R │ ├── fig_generalization_gap.R │ └── fig_consort_flow.R └── utils/ └── load_sidecar.R
configs/VISUALIZATION/ ├── figure_registry.yaml # Dimensions, data deps, output format └── colors.yaml # Okabe-Ito palette + domain colors

task.name: Python data export for R
task.spec: Biostatistics flow task: task_export_r_data() Exports to outputs/biostatistics/r_data/: - pairwise_results.json (Cohen's d, Cliff's delta, p-values) - anova_results.json (F-values, R², factor levels) - per_volume_data.json (metric × condition × fold × volume arrays) - rankings.json (per-metric condition ranks) - spec_curve.json (sorted specifications + indicators) - power_analysis.json (detectable d per metric) - variance_decomp.json (Friedman, ICC, Nemenyi) - test_generalization.json (MiniVess vs DeepVess per metric per condition)

task.name: Implement 10 publication figures
task.spec:
FIGURE REGISTRY (manuscript mapping):
Main figures (biostatistics scope): ┌──────┬───────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┐ │ Fig# │ Type │ R Script │ ├──────┼───────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────┤ │ F2 │ CONSORT volume flow diagram │ fig_consort_flow.R │ │ F4 │ Forest plot (Cohen's d + CIs) │ fig_forest_plot.R │ │ F5 │ Interaction plot (model×loss) │ fig_interaction_plot.R │ │ F6a │ Effect size heatmap (Cliff δ) │ fig_effect_size_heatmap.R │ │ F6b │ Specification curve │ fig_specification_curve.R │ │ F7 │ Generalization gap (train/test)│ fig_generalization_gap.R │ └──────┴───────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┘
Supplementary figures: ┌──────┬───────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┐ │ S1 │ Raincloud (per-volume dist) │ fig_raincloud.R │ │ S2 │ CD diagram (Nemenyi) │ fig_cd_diagram.R │ │ S3 │ Calibration reliability │ fig_calibration_reliability.R│ │ S4 │ Variance lollipop │ (matplotlib diagnostic OK) │ └──────┴───────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┘
Out of biostatistics scope (other skills): - F1: Architecture overview (React Flow) - F3: Pipeline protocol (React Flow)
Each figure: PDF (vector) + PNG (300 DPI) + JSON sidecar with: figure_id, generated_at, duckdb_sha256, git_sha, config_hash, r_session_info, data payload, output_files list

task.name: Sidecar-to-file assertion test
task.spec: Post-generation integration test: For every JSON sidecar in outputs/biostatistics/figures/: - Assert every output_files path exists - Assert file size > 0 - For PDFs: assert valid PDF header (%PDF-) - For PNGs: assert valid PNG header (89 50 4E 47) This catches the most common R rendering failure: silent empty files.

phase.name: LaTeX Table Generation
phase.description: Publication-ready LaTeX tables with booktabs + JSON sidecars. Tables generated from DuckDB + statistical results, not hardcoded.

task.name: Generate 8 table types
task.spec:
T1: Main comparison (conditions × metrics, BCa CIs, significance stars) T2: Hierarchical gatekeeping summary (co-primary + conditional secondary) T3: Effect sizes (Cohen's d, Cliff's delta, VDA per pair) T4: Variance decomposition (Friedman χ², p, ICC, CI, F-test) T5: Rankings per metric (mean rank, CD value) T6: ANOVA summary (lmerTest F, p, marginal R², per metric) T7: Cost appendix (GPU hours, wall-clock, cloud cost per condition) T8: Power analysis (detectable d at 80% power, per metric, per N scenario)
All tables: booktabs format, \input{}-ready for vascadia-results.tex. Each table: .tex file + .json sidecar (underlying data).

task.name: Table sidecar validation
task.spec: Same pattern as figure sidecars: Pydantic model for TableSidecar. Assert every .tex file has a corresponding .json sidecar. Assert sidecar data can regenerate the table content.

phase.name: Config-Driven Verification
phase.description: Verify the biostatistics pipeline is fully config-driven. No hardcoded alpha, seed, metric names, or factor names in code. AST-enforced.

task.name: AST scan for hardcoded values
task.spec: Extend test_no_hardcoded_alpha.py: - No alpha=0.05 in any biostatistics code - No seed=42 in any biostatistics code - No hardcoded metric names in statistical functions - No hardcoded factor names in ANOVA/condition key code All values must trace to BiostatisticsConfig fields.

task.name: Config mutation tests
task.spec: Test 1: Change alpha to 0.01 → pipeline runs, produces different significance decisions Test 2: Change primary_metric to dsc → pipeline runs, forest plot uses dsc Test 3: Add new metric "junction_f1" → pipeline includes it without code changes Test 4: Change seed to 0 → pipeline runs, results differ from seed=42 Test 5: Change seed to 42 → pipeline produces bit-identical results (reproducibility)

phase.name: Nature Protocols Compliance
phase.description: Generate Nature Protocols format artifacts: CONSORT diagram, timing annotations, provenance chain, limitations paragraph, pre-registration link.

task.name: CONSORT flow diagram data
task.spec:
Programmatically generated from experiment metadata (not hand-drawn): 70 MiniVess volumes → 3 folds (47 train / 23 val each) → 2 loss functions × 3 folds = 6 training runs → 2 post-training methods = 12 post-training runs → 1 ensemble strategy = 4 analysis conditions → biostatistics flow → 10 figures + 8 tables
Output: consort_data.json → consumed by fig_consort_flow.R

task.name: Timing annotations
task.spec: Extract from MLflow perf/* metrics: - Training: wall-clock per fold, per loss function - Post-training: wall-clock per method - Analysis: wall-clock for evaluation - Biostatistics: wall-clock for statistical engine + figure generation - Total pipeline: sum Format: JSON + LaTeX timing table for Procedure section.

task.name: Provenance chain figure
task.spec:
Lineage DAG showing exact data flow: MLflow mlruns/ → DuckDB (8 tables) → Statistical engine (7 analyses) → Figures (10) + Tables (8) → Manuscript sections
Generated as a Mermaid diagram (rendered to SVG/PNG via CLI). JSON sidecar with all SHA-256 hashes linking each step.

task.name: Limitations paragraph
task.spec:
Auto-generated from power analysis results: "With K=3 folds as blocks, the Friedman test achieves 80% power to detect only effect sizes > {d_detectable}. Per-volume stratified tests (N=23 per fold) provide substantially more power (detectable d = {d_volume}). This mini-experiment serves as a platform validation; the full factorial (720 conditions × 3 folds) will provide definitive statistical power."
Output: limitations_paragraph.tex (LaTeX fragment for inclusion)

task.name: Pre-registration link
task.spec: Add to manuscript methods: "The experimental design was pre-registered via git tag pre-registration/mini-experiment-v1 (SHA: {sha}) before experiments were executed. The factorial YAML, split configuration, and biostatistics configuration were frozen at this commit."

task.name: TRIPOD+AI item mapping
task.spec: Map every figure and table to TRIPOD+AI items: - T1 (comparison) → Item 23a (performance with CI) - T4 (variance) → Item 23b (heterogeneity) - F2 (CONSORT) → Item 6/20a (participant flow) - F7 (generalization) → Item 24 (external validation) - T8 (power) → Item 9a (sample size) Output: tripod_mapping.json

phase.name: Manuscript Integration
phase.description: Export all biostatistics artifacts to manuscript directory. Update KG with actual metric values. Link figures to manuscript sections.

task.name: Export to manuscript directory
task.spec:
Copy figures + tables to: /home/petteri/Dropbox/knowledge-hub/outputs/manuscripts/vasculature-mlops/ figures/biostatistics/ (PDF + PNG) tables/ (LaTeX .tex) data/ (JSON sidecars)
Naming convention: fig-biostat-{number}-{slug}.pdf

task.name: Update knowledge-graph
task.spec:
knowledge-graph/manuscript/results.yaml: R3a: Update with actual metric values from mini-experiment - cbdice_cldice: DSC=actual, clDice=actual, MASD=actual - dice_ce: DSC=actual, clDice=actual, MASD=actual Status: DATA_AVAILABLE (not BLOCKED)
knowledge-graph/domains/manuscript.yaml: Update figure assignments with actual file paths

task.name: Lineage manifest
task.spec: JSON manifest linking: DuckDB (SHA-256) → each figure/table → manuscript section → TRIPOD item Logged to MLflow as artifact of biostatistics run. Satisfies IEC 62304 §8 traceability requirement.

risks.risk: Config drift between design and execution. Mitigation: git tag freezes config.
Statistical pipeline produces wrong results on real data. Mitigation: synthetic data with known ground truth validates pipeline before real experiment.
R package version drift breaks figures. Mitigation: renv lockfile committed.
DuckDB schema mismatch between analysis flow output and biostatistics input. Mitigation: contract tests run on real data before biostatistics flow starts.
BCa bootstrap with small N produces unreliable CIs. Mitigation: automatic fallback to percentile when N < 20.
DeepVess data not available locally. Mitigation: DVC-versioned, downloaded as part of Phase 3 prerequisites.

success_criteria.criterion: All 68+ existing biostatistics tests pass (make test-staging)
New tests: stratified permutation, mixed-effects, gatekeeping, contract, config-driven (target +30 tests)
Biostatistics flow runs end-to-end on real DuckDB, produces all figures + tables
Every figure has JSON sidecar with valid schema, referenced files exist
Every table has JSON sidecar with underlying data
Power analysis produces quantified limitations paragraph
Pre-registration git tag exists before experiment execution
Zero hardcoded alpha, seed, metric names, factor names in code (AST-verified)
Seed=42 produces bit-identical results across runs (reproducibility)
Config mutation: change alpha/metric/seed → pipeline adapts without code changes

iteration.reviewer: Pooling bug, invalid BCa for N=3, ANOVA misspecification, missing power analysis.
DeepVess optional, no Procedure/Timing/CONSORT, platform figures absent.
3 data source paths, no Docker detail, no contract tests.
Stratified permutation underspecified, mixed-model formula needs pinning, power analysis target unclear, volume-pairing guarantee needed.
R vs matplotlib ambiguous, missing calibration + generalization gap figures, pre-registration needs git tag, no figure numbering.
Missing Phase 3→4 gate, no round-trip property test, no sidecar-file assertion.

iteration.description: All iteration 2 feedback incorporated into this final plan: - D3: Stratified within-fold permutation specified as primary - D4: Mixed-model formula pinned (random intercept only + lmerTest) - Task 4.2: Power analysis with design effect correction - Task 1.1: Volume-pairing via (fold_id, volume_id) join - D1: R/ggplot2 committed as primary, matplotlib for diagnostics - Task 5.4: Calibration reliability + generalization gap figures added - D5: Pre-registration via git tag - Task 5.4: Explicit figure numbering (F2-F7, S1-S4) - Task 3.5: Phase 3→4 validation gate added - Task 1.5: Round-trip property test (Hypothesis) - Task 5.5: Sidecar-to-file assertion test - Task 2.2: Simulation validation (positive control) - Task 5.1: R package pinning via renv.lock
